senses.definition: to hit the lowest point before things get better

examples.x: The company's profits bottomed out last year but are on the rise now.
Confidence in the project bottomed out, but now there's renewed enthusiasm.
The economy bottomed out during the recession, but signs of recovery are emerging.

senses.definition: to expand by exploring new areas, options, or opportunities

examples.x: She is eager to branch out professionally and explore new career paths.
The organization is looking to branch out and explore new opportunities.
The artist is considering branching out beyond traditional mediums.
The company wants to branch out into international markets.

senses.definition: (of an infectious disease) to start and spread within a community

examples.x: The unsupervised food handling broke out cases of foodborne illnesses.
The infected person inadvertently broke out the virus in the crowded gathering.

senses.definition: to prolong or extend a situation, event, or process, often unnecessarily

examples.x: The speaker seemed to drag out the presentation, causing some attendees to lose interest.
The negotiations were dragged out due to disagreements on minor details.
The legal proceedings were dragged out, increasing the stress on all parties involved.
The teacher decided not to drag out the class unnecessarily and concluded the lesson on time.
The management promised not to drag out the decision-making process for the new project.

senses.definition: to extend in time, length, or duration, often longer than necessary

examples.x: The speaker decided to draw out the presentation, providing additional details and examples.
The negotiations were drawn out due to disagreements on key issues.
In an effort to be thorough, the teacher chose to draw out the lesson, covering each topic in depth.
The artist carefully drew out the painting process, ensuring every detail was perfected.
The interviewee tended to draw out responses, elaborating on each answer with anecdotes and explanations.

senses.definition: to extend one's hand or an object toward someone, often to give or offer something to them

examples.x: She held out her hand, offering a warm handshake to greet the visitor.
The child held out a bouquet of flowers to her mother as a surprise.
For the signing of the contract, he held out a pen.

senses.definition: to extend outward from a surface or object

examples.x: The rocky cliff juts out over the edge of the sea, providing a breathtaking view.
The branch of the tree juts out and provides a perfect spot for the birds to perch.

senses.definition: to fill an arena to its capacity

examples.x: The rock band's performance packed out the arena within hours of ticket sales.
The magic show packed out the theater every night for a week.
The charity event packed out the hotel ballroom.
We were amazed to see the tiny club packed out for the local band's gig.
Every time she gives a lecture, she packs out the auditorium.

senses.definition: to gradually lose strength or intensity

examples.x: As the sun dipped below the horizon, the daylight began to fade out, giving way to the evening.
The enthusiasm of the crowd started to fade out as the disappointing outcome of the game became apparent.
The echoes of laughter slowly faded out as the comedy show concluded, leaving a sense of contentment in the audience.
The once vibrant colors of the painting began to fade out over the years due to exposure to sunlight.
The effectiveness of the pain medication started to fade out, requiring a higher dosage for continued relief.

senses.definition: to gradually end or fade away, often due to becoming weakened

examples.x: The trail peters out about half a mile up the mountain, making it difficult to follow.
Our conversations often peter out into awkward silences.

senses.definition: to extend a process, activity, or situation

examples.x: The politician spun out the debate, responding to each question with lengthy explanations.
The teacher spun out the lesson by incorporating additional examples and interactive activities.
The speaker spun out the presentation by elaborating on each point in great detail.

senses.definition: to separate a group of things and arrange or place them over a large area

examples.x: The event planner instructed the staff to spread out the chairs in the conference room for the meeting.
The librarian suggested spreading out the study tables in the library for a more comfortable studying environment.

senses.definition: to extend something to its full extent

examples.x: Please stretch out the measuring tape and let me know how long the table is.
People in the back of the crowded room had to stretch out their necks to see the presentation on the screen.

senses.definition: to decrease the number or density of something

examples.x: The manager decided to thin out the excessive paperwork in the office.
The librarian thinned out the book collection to make space for new acquisitions.
The supervisor decided to thin out the employees in the department to improve efficiency.
